Hemothorax
Hemothorax which was felt to be secondary to intercostal vessel injury in the setting of his RFA
This required VATS with 2 Liters of old blood removed, and the placement of three chest tubes

the patients CXRs showed healing, and he began to ambulate easily with physical therapy
initially recomended for subacute rehab placement, however an amenable facility could not be found.
He worked more intensely with PT who cleared him to go home with home PT.
